compiled_state.title: Update project docs to reflect WORKTREE_TRACKING comment lifecycle
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Updates existing project documentation to describe the WORKTREE_TRACKING comment lifecycle, autonomous resume logic, and debug-everything gap so future agents have accurate awareness of the tracking system.

**Why**: After Stories A–D ship, CLAUDE.md and other docs will be stale. Future agents and developers need accurate documentation to understand the tracking comment format, the resume decision logic, and the known gap with debug-everything.

**Scope**:
- IN: Updates to CLAUDE.md (hook architecture section: describe WORKTREE_TRACKING lifecycle; ticket system section: note the comment types); sprint/fix-bug workflow docs if they describe resume behavior; .claude/docs/KNOWN-ISSUES.md if a known limitation (e.g., debug-everything gap) warrants a notice
- OUT: New documentation files (update existing docs only); implementation details in docs

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md accurately describes the WORKTREE_TRACKING:start/complete/landed comment lifecycle, the autonomous resume logic behavior in sprint and fix-bug, and notes the debug-everything gap as a known limitation requiring follow-up
  ← Satisfies: Documentation reflects the full set of tracking comment SCs delivered by Stories A–D

## Considerations

- Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for documentation formatting, structure, and conventions when writing documentation updates

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for documentation formatting, structure, and conventions.
comments.author: Claude
